career_summary: MS Data Science candidate at UVA with hands-on experience building ML pipelines, NLP models, and data dashboards. Background in data analysis and statistical modeling with Python. Looking to apply semantic ML and LLM tooling in production environments.

work_history.title: Data Analyst
accomplishments: Reduced monthly report generation time from 3 hours to 20 minutes by rewriting SQL aggregation queries and automating delivery with Python scripts
Built Tableau dashboards tracking 15 KPIs for the clinical operations team, used weekly by 40+ stakeholders
Identified a data pipeline bug that was silently dropping ~8% of claim records; fix recovered $2.1M in previously untracked costs
Cleaned and merged 5 disparate datasets (3.2M rows) for an actuarial model, improving model accuracy by 12%

projects.name: AI-Powered Resume & Job Match Engine
projects.description: End-to-end NLP system that semantically scores resumes against job descriptions using sentence-transformers and spaCy NER, generates optimized LaTeX resumes, and exposes a CLI interface.
outcomes: Built semantic scoring pipeline achieving meaningful differentiation between matched and unmatched resume-JD pairs
Implemented brace-balanced LaTeX parser to correctly extract nested commands from real resume templates
Designed modular engine/ architecture with zero FastAPI/DB dependencies so core logic is independently testable
56/56 pytest tests passing across scorer, extractor, and LaTeX builder modules

projects.name: LLM Document Q&A with RAG
projects.description: Retrieval-augmented generation pipeline that lets users ask natural language questions over a corpus of PDF documents.
outcomes: Reduced hallucination rate from ~35% (pure LLM) to ~8% (RAG) on internal evaluation set of 50 questions
Processed and indexed 200+ research papers in under 5 minutes using async chunking pipeline
Deployed as a FastAPI service with streaming responses, reducing perceived latency by 60%

projects.name: Healthcare Claims Anomaly Detection
projects.description: Time-series anomaly detection model on insurance claims data to flag unusual billing patterns.
outcomes: Achieved 91% precision at 0.1% false positive rate on held-out test set
Flagged 340 anomalous claims in first production month, escalated to compliance team
Reduced manual review queue by 23% by prioritizing high-confidence anomaly flags
